revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:2 nguŏk 15 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:2月15日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 2월 15일]][[zh-min-nan:2 goe̍h 15 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:二月十五]][[zh:二月十五]]
{{Not to be confused with|February 15|qq=February 15}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=二月十五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 2월 15일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar February|qq=}} '''February 15''' is the 15th day of the month {{ill|February (Lunisolar)|ja|2月 (旧暦)|lt=February (Kisaragi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 2nd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu|1=Butsumetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10886789}}. == Events == * {{ill|Hakuchi (era)|en|Hakuchi (era)|qid=Q540118|lt=Hakuchi|1=Hakuchi (era)}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|650|en|650|qid=Q30179|lt=650|1=650}} {{ill|March 22|en|March 22|qid=Q2435|lt=March 22|1=March 22}}) – Due to the presentation of a white {{ill|Kiji|en|Kiji|qid=Q6406156|lt=pheasant|1=Kiji}} from the official of Shishido Province, the era name was changed from {{ill|Taika (era)|en|Taika (era)|qid=Q1077930|lt=Taika|1=Taika (era)}} to Hakuchi by edict. * {{ill|Wadō (era)|en|Wadō (era)|qid=Q1373026|lt=Wadō|1=Wadō (era)}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|708|en|708|qid=Q23511|lt=708|1=708}} {{ill|March 11|en|March 11|qid=Q2398|lt=March 11|1=March 11}}) – {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|qid=Q233224|lt=Emperor Genmei|1=Empress Genmei}} issued an edict regarding the construction of Heijō-kyō. * {{ill|Chōji|en|Chōji|qid=Q1150870|lt=Chōji|1=Chōji}} 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1105|en|1105|qid=Q13719|lt=1105|1=1105}} {{ill|March 3|en|March 3|qid=Q2391|lt=March 3|1=March 3}}) – {{ill|Fujiwara no Kiyohira|en|Fujiwara no Kiyohira|qid=Q5360232|lt=Fujiwara Kiyohira|1=Fujiwara no Kiyohira}} established the first Inndaibō-ji (later known as {{ill|Chūson-ji|en|Chūson-ji|qid=Q2660144|lt=Chūson-ji|1=Chūson-ji}}) in the Oshu region (Mutsu Province, 【Hiraizumi】) to commemorate the war dead of the 【Later Three Years War】. * {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1624|en|1624|qid=Q6821|lt=1624|1=1624}} {{ill|April 2|en|April 2|qid=Q2511|lt=April 2|1=April 2}}) – A man named Saruwaka Kanzaburō (the first generation of {{ill|Nakamura Kanzaburō|ja|初代中村勘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Kanzaburō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) left Yamashiro Province for Edo and established the Saruwaka-za (later known as Nakamura-za) on Kyōbashi, marking the beginning of Edo {{ill|Kabuki|en|Kabuki|qid=Q199701|lt=kabuki|1=Kabuki}}. * {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|qid=Q1146135|lt=Keian|1=Keian}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1648|en|1648|qid=Q6941|lt=1648|1=1648}} {{ill|April 7|en|April 7|qid=Q2505|lt=April 7|1=April 7}}) – The era name was changed from {{ill|Shōhō|en|Shōhō|qid=Q1146192|lt=Shōhō|1=Shōhō}} to Keian. * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|qid=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei|1=Bunsei}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1825|en|1825|qid=Q6997|lt=1825|1=1825}} {{ill|April 3|en|April 3|qid=Q2509|lt=April 3|1=April 3}}) – The Tokugawa shogunate issued the edict known as the {{ill|Edict to Repel Foreign Vessels|en|Edict to Repel Foreign Vessels|qid=Q465185|lt=Ikokusen Uchi Harai Rei|1=Edict to Repel Foreign Vessels}} (also called the Muni-nen Uchi Harai Rei). * {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|qid=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1868|en|1868|qid=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} {{ill|March 8|en|March 8|qid=Q2395|lt=March 8|1=March 8}}) – The Sakai Incident occurred, in which soldiers from the Tosa Domain killed and injured French naval personnel. == Births == * {{ill|Hōen|en|Hōen|qid=Q1193929|lt=Hoen|1=Hōen}} 7th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1141|en|1141|qid=Q19613|lt=1141|1=1141}} {{ill|March 24|en|March 24|qid=Q2453|lt=March 24|1=March 24}}) – {{ill|Kumagai Naozane|en|Kumagai Naozane|qid=Q3335867|lt=Kumagaya Naomichi|1=Kumagai Naozane}}, a military commander (died {{ill|1208|en|1208|qid=Q5238|lt=1208|1=1208}})<ref>「四十八巻伝」27では、蓮生は{{ill|Ken'ei|en|Ken'ei|qid=Q1194153|lt=Ken'ei|1=Ken'ei}} 2nd year 9月4日（{{ill|1207|en|1207|qid=Q5232|lt=1207|1=1207}} {{ill|September 27|en|September 27|qid=Q2884|lt=September 27|1=September 27}}）に往生したとある。</ref>. * {{ill|En'ō|en|En'ō|qid=Q1193942|lt=En'ō|1=En'ō}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1239|en|1239|qid=Q5434|lt=1239|1=1239}} {{ill|March 21|en|March 21|qid=Q2433|lt=March 21|1=March 21}}) – {{ill|Ippen|en|Ippen|qid=Q1056232|lt=Ippen|1=Ippen}}, a monk and founder of the Jishū (侶) branch of {{ill|Ji-shu|en|Ji-shu|qid=Q696966|lt=Ji-shū|1=Ji-shu}} (died {{ill|1289|en|1289|qid=Q5551|lt=1289|1=1289}}). * {{ill|Enkyō (Edo period)|en|Enkyō (Edo period)|qid=Q1183728|lt=Enkyō|1=Enkyō (Edo period)}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1745|en|1745|qid=Q7586|lt=1745|1=1745}} {{ill|March 17|en|March 17|qid=Q2419|lt=March 17|1=March 17}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Shigeyoshi|en|Tokugawa Shigeyoshi|qid=Q3530495|lt=Tokugawa Shigeyoshi|1=Tokugawa Shigeyoshi}}, the first head of the Shimizu-Tokugawa family (died {{ill|1795|en|1795|qid=Q7815|lt=1795|1=1795}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|qid=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei|1=Bunsei}} 12nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1829|en|1829|qid=Q7572|lt=1829|1=1829}} {{ill|March 19|en|March 19|qid=Q2424|lt=March 19|1=March 19}}) – {{ill|Uryu Iwako|en|Uryu Iwako|qid=Q7901613|lt=Uryū Iwako|1=Uryu Iwako}}, a social entrepreneur (died {{ill|1897|en|1897|qid=Q7847|lt=1897|1=1897}}). * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|qid=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 9th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1838|en|1838|qid=Q7612|lt=1838|1=1838}} {{ill|March 10|en|March 10|qid=Q2397|lt=March 10|1=March 10}}) – {{ill|Shimaji Mokurai|ja|島地黙雷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimaji Mokurai|1=Shimaji Mokurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6605997}}, a monk of the Jōdo Shinshū Honganji-ha sect (died {{ill|1911|en|1911|qid=Q2076|lt=1911|1=1911}}). * {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|qid=Q1327259|lt=Ansei|1=Ansei}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1858|en|1858|qid=Q7684|lt=1858|1=1858}} {{ill|March 29|en|March 29|qid=Q2459|lt=March 29|1=March 29}}) – {{ill|Wainai Sadayuki|en|Wainai Sadayuki|qid=Q7960142|lt=Waiuchi Sadayuki|1=Wainai Sadayuki}}, an aquaculture entrepreneur (died {{ill|1922|en|1922|qid=Q2165|lt=1922|1=1922}}). == Deaths == * In {{ill|544 BC|en|544 BC|qid=Q243440|lt=544 BC|1=544 BC}} (various legends) – {{ill|The Buddha|en|The Buddha|qid=Q9441|lt=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|1=The Buddha}}, the founder of Buddhism (traditionally said to have died in 624 BC, according to southern legends); see also {{ill|Parinirvana Day|en|Parinirvana Day|qid=Q4115787|lt=Nirvana-kai|1=Parinirvana Day}}. * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Ōjin Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}} 41st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|310|en|310|qid=Q32427|lt=310|1=310}} {{ill|March 31|en|March 31|qid=Q2461|lt=March 31|1=March 31}}) – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, the 15th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}} (traditionally born in 200). * {{ill|Manju (era)|en|Manju (era)|qid=Q1194155|lt=Manju|1=Manju (era)}} 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1025|en|1025|qid=Q19267|lt=1025|1=1025}} {{ill|March 17|en|March 17|qid=Q2419|lt=March 17|1=March 17}}) – {{ill|Watanabe no Tsuna|en|Watanabe no Tsuna|qid=Q6582069|lt=Watanabe Tsuna|1=Watanabe no Tsuna}}, a military commander and one of the Four Guardian Kings of Minamoto no Yorimitsu (died {{ill|953|en|953|qid=Q23586|lt=953|1=953}}). * {{ill|Daiji|ja|大治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiji|1=Q10936305|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10936305}} 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1127|en|1127|qid=Q23827|lt=1127|1=1127}} {{ill|March 29|en|March 29|qid=Q2459|lt=March 29|1=March 29}}) – {{ill|Minamoto no Masanori|ja|源雅実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Masanori|1=Minamoto no Masazane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11564335}}, awarded the junior first Rank (従一位) and served as {{ill|Minister (government)|en|Minister (government)|qid=Q83307|lt=Daijō-daijin|1=Minister (government)}}. * {{ill|Kannō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannō|qq=}} 2nd year / {{ill|Zhengping|en|Zhengping|qid=Q11123413|lt=Shōhei|1=Zhengping}} 5th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1350|en|1350|qid=Q6094|lt=1350|1=1350}} {{ill|March 23|en|March 23|qid=Q2445|lt=March 23|1=March 23}}) – {{ill|Yoshida Kenkō|en|Yoshida Kenkō|qid=Q372772|lt=Tombeki Kanekatsu|1=Yoshida Kenkō}}, a waka poet and essayist. * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|qid=Q730266|lt=Bunka|1=Bunkyū}} 14th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1817|en|1817|qid=Q6958|lt=1817|1=1817}} {{ill|April 1|en|April 1|qid=Q2510|lt=April 1|1=April 1}}) – {{ill|Nakai Riken|en|Nakai Riken|qid=Q6563588|lt=Nakai Riken|1=Nakai Riken}}, a Confucian scholar ({{ill|Ru|ja|儒学者|zh|儒生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ru|1=Ru (Confucian scholar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125199930}}) (died {{ill|1732|en|1732|qid=Q6966|lt=1732|1=1732}}). == Commemorations == * [[Nirvana-kai|涅槃会]] * Also commemorated are the death anniversaries of {{ill|Saigyō|en|Saigyō|qid=Q224689|lt=西行|1=Saigyō}} and [[En'i|円位]] – although Saigyō’s recorded death is February 16 (旧暦), the tradition based on his poem "願はくは花の下にて春死なむそのきさらぎの望月の頃" designates the 15th as his death anniversary.
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|2|14|2|16|1|15|3|2|0215|qq=}} ==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}
[[zh-min-nan:2 goe̍h 15 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:二月十五]]
{{translated page|ja|2月15日 (旧暦)|version=100114873|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[cdo:2 nguŏk 15 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[de:February 15 (Lunisolar)]]
[[ko:음력 2월 15일]] [[ja:2月15日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:二月十五]]
[[ar:February 15 (Lunisolar)]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:2 goe̍h 15 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:二月十五]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:2 goe̍h 15 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:二月十五]] {{wikidata link|Q839233}} [[Category:Butsumetsu]] [[Category:Days 15 of Japanese Lunar months]] [[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] [[Category:deprecated start]] [[Category:Lunisolar February]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
